message:
feat(indexer/responsiveness): governor telemetry, worker-pool integration, and search-latency validation test

Builds on the loadavg-aware controller (85ef6146) by surfacing *why* the
governor made each decision and by extending the governor clamp to the
lexical rebuild worker pool, the Tantivy writer parallelism hint, and
page-prep fan-out.

indexer/responsiveness.rs (+624/-66)
  - New GovernorDecisionReason enum (snake_case serde) with variants:
    Disabled, Severe, Pressured, PressuredFloorHold, HealthyHold,
    HealthyGrow, HealthyCeilingHold. Robot-mode consumers can switch
    on a stable string vocabulary rather than parsing free-form text.
  - New telemetry ring buffer (VecDeque, capped at 128 decisions ~=
    4 minutes of history at the default 2-second tick) kept behind a
    Mutex plus atomics. telemetry_snapshot() returns a serializable
    view: current_capacity_pct, healthy_streak, shrink/grow counts,
    ticks_total, disabled_via_env, last_snapshot, last_reason,
    recent_decisions. Structure stays under 16 KB.
  - HealthSnapshot now derives serde::Serialize so snapshots can be
    embedded directly in structured-output payloads.
  - effective_worker_count(raw) helper so callers that size thread
    pools up-front can apply the live capacity clamp without threading
    the governor handle through every constructor.

indexer/mod.rs
  - build_lexical_rebuild_worker_pool() now sizes the pool via
    effective_worker_count(); downstream consumers still apply their
    own governor clamp on top of their own hard caps so you can never
    accidentally exceed the pool size.
  - lexical_rebuild_pipeline_settings_snapshot() switches the tantivy
    writer parallelism hint to the *_governed variant so pipeline
    telemetry reflects actual fan-out.
  - Page-prep default worker parallelism ceiling lifted from 8 to 16.
    The old 8 was a hard guardrail against runaway fanout on big
    hosts; now that the governor applies a live capacity clamp on top,
    16 keeps producer fetch/prep overlap meaningful on 32+ core
    builders without saturating the DB, while on small hosts the
    div_ceil(2) factor prevents oversubscription.

indexer/semantic.rs
  - Remove defensive .max(self.batch_size) on the rayon prep window:
    with_batch_size() and resolved_default_batch_size() both guarantee
    batch_size >= 1, so saturating_mul(4) is always >= batch_size.
    Dead clamp, removed.

lib.rs
  - print_robot_docs() gains eleven new CASS_RESPONSIVENESS_* /
    CASS_STREAMING_CONSUMER_* / CASS_SEMANTIC_* env vars so operators
    can actually discover the governor tunables.
  - run_health() --json payload now includes a responsiveness field
    populated from telemetry_snapshot(); cheap (in-memory ring + a
    few atomics, no fallible I/O).
  - build_response_schemas() adds a responsiveness object schema with
    nested properties for snapshot, reason vocabulary, and the
    recent_decisions array, including at_elapsed_ms and prev/next
    capacity fields. Operators reading --json can schema-validate.

tests/search_latency_under_indexing.rs (new, 294 lines, #[ignore])
  - First slice of the "interactive latency stays sane while cass
    indexes in the background" scenario (bead
    coding_agent_session_search-d2qix). Asserts that an active Tantivy
    writer (seeding + periodic commits) does not starve a foreground
    SearchClient. Thresholds intentionally generous (p95 <= 750 ms
    pressured, <= 300 ms idle) so the test works even on small dev
    boxes — regression signal is the *delta*, not the absolute number.
    Pulls governor telemetry at the end so the run log captures
    capacity decisions alongside the timing histograms. #[ignore] by
    default because it is wall-clock sensitive and burns ~2-5s CPU
    per run; opt-in with `cargo test --test
    search_latency_under_indexing -- --ignored --nocapture`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
